commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=3bb8c8a0475df8e6e9a9b6774a4c5509caef8e34)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Perplexity and Tavily successful web-search JSON response parsing to read through a 16 MiB bounded response helper instead of calling response.json() directly.

PR surface: Source +26. Total +26 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main has unbounded response.json() calls in the Perplexity and Tavily success JSON helpers. I did not run a live oversized streaming repro in this read-only checkout.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed for the shared-helper rewrite and real behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not queue an automated repair because the proof gate requires the contributor's real setup evidence.

target.title: fix(search): bound perplexity and tavily web-search JSON response reads
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Perplexity and Tavily web-search success responses were read via an unbounded `await response.json()` inside local helper functions (`readPerplexityJsonResponse`, `readTavilyJsonResponse`). A misbehaving or hostile search endpoint could stream an arbitrarily large body into memory before parsing, potentially causing OOM. Both providers route through external network endpoints that are not fully trusted.

The same gap was already fixed for `exa` (#96038) and `parallel` (#96035) in the ongoing response-limit campaign. This PR applies the same fix to the two remaining bundled web-search providers.

The fix routes the success body through the shared `readResponseWithLimit` helper (re-exported via `openclaw/plugin-sdk/response-limit-runtime`) under a 16 MiB cap, cancelling the stream on overflow. Overflow produces a labelled error that the existing error path propagates to the caller.

## Evidence

Behavior addressed: Perplexity and Tavily web-search JSON response reads were unbounded; a hostile or malfunctioning endpoint could exhaust memory.

Real environment tested: macOS, Node 22, local source checkout.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/perplexity/src --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/tavily/src --reporter=verbose
```

Evidence after fix:
```
Test Files  1 passed (1)   # perplexity
Test Files  1 passed (1)   # tavily
```

Observed result after fix: All existing tests pass. The `readPerplexityJsonResponse` and `readTavilyJsonResponse` helpers now cancel the stream and throw a labelled error if the response body exceeds 16 MiB, matching the pattern used by `exa` and `parallel`.

What was not tested: Live Perplexity/Tavily API calls with an oversized response (no hostile test endpoint available locally); overflow path relies on the shared `readResponseWithLimit` unit tests and the pattern proven in #96038/#96035.

AI-assisted.
target.author: hugenshen
